10	from mlc_llm.serve import engine_utils
11	from mlc_llm.serve.engine_base import (
12	    EngineConfig,
13	    EngineMetrics,
14	    _check_engine_config,
15	    _parse_models,
16	    _process_model_args,
17	    _query_engine_metrics,
18	    detect_device,
19	)
20	from mlc_llm.tokenizers import Tokenizer
21	
22	
23	class EngineState:
24	    sync_queue: queue.Queue
25	
26	    def get_request_stream_callback(self) -> Callable[[str], None]:
27	        # ChatCompletionStreamResponse
28	
29	        def _callback(chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str: str) -> None:
30	            self._sync_request_stream_callback(chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str)
31	
32	        return _callback
33	
34	    def _sync_request_stream_callback(self, chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str: str) -> None:
35	        # Put the delta outputs to the queue in the unblocking way.
36	        self.sync_queue.put_nowait(chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str)
37	
38	    def handle_chat_completion(
39	        self, ffi: dict, request_json_str: str, include_usage: bool, request_id: str
40	    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
41	        """Helper class to handle chat completion
42	
43	        Note
44	        ----
45	        ffi is explicitly passed in to avoid cylic dependency
46	        as ffi will capture EngineState
47	        """
48	        self.sync_queue = queue.Queue()
49	
50	        ffi["chat_completion"](request_json_str, request_id)
51	
52	        try:
53	            last_chunk_arrived = False
54	            while not last_chunk_arrived:
55	                chat_completion_responses_json_str = self.sync_queue.get()
56	                chat_completion_responses_list = json.loads(chat_completion_responses_json_str)
57	                for chat_completion_response_json_dict in chat_completion_responses_list:
58	                    chat_completion_response = (
59	                        openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse.model_validate(
60	                            chat_completion_response_json_dict
61	                        )
62	                    )
63	                    # the chunk with usage is always the last chunk
64	                    if chat_completion_response.usage is not None:
65	                        if include_usage:
66	                            yield chat_completion_response
67	                        last_chunk_arrived = True
68	                        break
69	                    yield chat_completion_response
70	        except Exception as exception:
71	            ffi["abort"](request_id)
72	            raise exception
73	
74	
75	class BackgroundLoops:
76	    """Helper class to keep track of background loops"""
77	
78	    def __init__(self, ffi: dict):
79	        self._ffi = ffi
80	        # important: avoid self reference in closure
81	        background_loop = self._ffi["run_background_loop"]
82	        background_stream_back_loop = self._ffi["run_background_stream_back_loop"]
83	
84	        # Create the background engine-driving thread and start the loop.
85	        self._background_loop_thread: threading.Thread = threading.Thread(target=background_loop)
86	        self._background_stream_back_loop_thread: threading.Thread = threading.Thread(
87	            target=background_stream_back_loop
88	        )
89	        self._background_loop_thread.start()
90	        self._background_stream_back_loop_thread.start()
91	        self._terminated = False
92	
93	    def __del__(self):
94	        self.terminate()
95	
96	    def terminate(self):
97	        if self._terminated:
98	            return
99	        self._terminated = True
100	        self._ffi["exit_background_loop"]()
101	        self._background_loop_thread.join()
102	        self._background_stream_back_loop_thread.join()
103	
104	
105	class Completions:
106	    """Completions class to be compatible with OpenAI API"""
107	
108	    _ffi: dict
109	    _state: EngineState
110	    _background_loops: BackgroundLoops
111	
112	    def __init__(self, ffi: dict, state: EngineState, background_loops: BackgroundLoops):
113	        self._ffi = ffi
114	        self._state = state
115	        self._background_loops = background_loops
116	
117	    def create(
118	        self,
119	        *,
120	        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
121	        model: Optional[str] = None,
122	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
123	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
124	        logprobs: bool = False,
125	        top_logprobs: int = 0,
126	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
127	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
128	        n: int = 1,
129	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
130	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
131	        stream: bool = True,
132	        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
133	        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
134	        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
135	        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
136	        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
137	        user: Optional[str] = None,
138	        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
139	        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
140	        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
141	    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
142	        if request_id is None:
143	            request_id = f"chatcmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"
144	        debug_config = extra_body.get("debug_config", None) if extra_body is not None else None
145	        if not stream:
146	            raise ValueError("JSONFFIEngine only support stream=True")
147	        request = openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest(
148	            messages=[
149	                openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionMessage.model_validate(message)
150	                for message in messages
151	            ],
152	            model=model,
153	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
154	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
155	            logprobs=logprobs,
156	            top_logprobs=top_logprobs,
157	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
158	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
159	            n=n,
160	            seed=seed,
161	            stop=stop,
162	            stream=stream,
163	            stream_options=(
164	                openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
165	                if stream_options is not None
166	                else None
167	            ),
168	            temperature=temperature,
169	            top_p=top_p,
170	            tools=(
171	                [openai_api_protocol.ChatTool.model_validate(tool) for tool in tools]
172	                if tools is not None
173	                else None
174	            ),
175	            tool_choice=tool_choice,
176	            user=user,
177	            response_format=(
178	                openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
179	                if response_format is not None
180	                else None
181	            ),
182	            debug_config=(
183	                debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
184	                if debug_config is not None
185	                else None
186	            ),
187	        )
188	        chatcmpl_generator = self._state.handle_chat_completion(
189	            self._ffi,
190	            request.model_dump_json(by_alias=True),
191	            include_usage=(
192	                request.stream_options is not None and request.stream_options.include_usage
193	            ),
194	            request_id=request_id,
195	        )
196	        for response in chatcmpl_generator:
197	            yield response
198	
199	
200	class Chat:
201	    """Chat class to be compatible with OpenAI API"""
202	
203	    completions: Completions
204	
205	    def __init__(self, ffi: dict, state: EngineState, background_loops: BackgroundLoops):
206	        self.completions = Completions(ffi, state, background_loops)
207	
208	
209	class JSONFFIEngine:
210	    chat: Chat
211	
212	    def __init__(
213	        self,
214	        model: str,
215	        device: Union[str, tvm.runtime.Device] = "auto",
216	        *,
217	        model_lib: Optional[str] = None,
218	        mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"] = "local",
219	        engine_config: Optional[EngineConfig] = None,
220	    ) -> None:
221	        # - Check the fields fields of `engine_config`.
222	        if engine_config is None:
223	            engine_config = EngineConfig()
224	        _check_engine_config(model, model_lib, mode, engine_config)
225	
226	        # - Initialize model loading info.
227	        models = _parse_models(model, model_lib, engine_config.additional_models)
228	        if isinstance(device, str):
229	            device = detect_device(device)
230	        assert isinstance(device, tvm.runtime.Device)
231	        model_args = _process_model_args(models, device, engine_config)[0]
232	
233	        # - Load the raw model config into dict
234	        for i, model_info in enumerate(models):
235	            model_info.model_lib = model_args[i][1]
236	
237	        # - Initialize engine state and engine.
238	        self._state = EngineState()
239	        module = tvm.get_global_func("mlc.json_ffi.CreateJSONFFIEngine", allow_missing=False)()
240	        self._ffi = {
241	            key: module[key]
242	            for key in [
243	                "init_background_engine",
244	                "reload",
245	                "unload",
246	                "reset",
247	                "chat_completion",
248	                "abort",
249	                "run_background_loop",
250	                "run_background_stream_back_loop",
251	                "exit_background_loop",
252	            ]
253	        }
254	        self.tokenizer = Tokenizer(model_args[0][0])
255	        self._background_loops = BackgroundLoops(self._ffi)
256	
257	        engine_config.model = model_args[0][0]
258	        engine_config.model_lib = model_args[0][1]
259	        engine_config.additional_models = model_args[1:]
260	        engine_config.mode = mode
261	        self.engine_config = engine_config
262	
263	        self._ffi["init_background_engine"](
264	            device.dlpack_device_type(),
265	            device.index,
266	            self._state.get_request_stream_callback(),
267	        )
268	        self._ffi["reload"](self.engine_config.asjson())
269	
270	        self.chat = Chat(self._ffi, self._state, self._background_loops)
271	
272	    def metrics(self) -> EngineMetrics:
273	        """Get the engine metrics."""
274	        return _query_engine_metrics(self)
275	
276	    def _raw_chat_completion(
277	        self, request_json_str: str, include_usage: bool, request_id: str
278	    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
279	        """Raw chat completion API"""
280	        return self._state.handle_chat_completion(
281	            self._ffi, request_json_str, include_usage, request_id
282	        )
283	
284	    def terminate(self):
285	        """Explicitly terminate the engine"""
286	        self._background_loops.terminate()
287	
288	    def _test_reload(self):
289	        self._ffi["reload"](self.engine_config.asjson())
290	
291	    def _test_reset(self):
292	        self._ffi["reset"]()
293	
294	    def _test_unload(self):
295	        self._ffi["unload"]()

import json
import queue
import threading
from collections.abc import Iterator
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Literal, Optional, Union  # noqa: UP035

import tvm

from mlc_llm.protocol import debug_protocol, openai_api_protocol
from mlc_llm.serve import engine_utils
from mlc_llm.serve.engine_base import (
    EngineConfig,
    EngineMetrics,
    _check_engine_config,
    _parse_models,
    _process_model_args,
    _query_engine_metrics,
    detect_device,
)
from mlc_llm.tokenizers import Tokenizer


class EngineState:
    sync_queue: queue.Queue

    def get_request_stream_callback(self) -> Callable[[str], None]:
        # ChatCompletionStreamResponse

        def _callback(chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str: str) -> None:
            self._sync_request_stream_callback(chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str)

        return _callback

    def _sync_request_stream_callback(self, chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str: str) -> None:
        # Put the delta outputs to the queue in the unblocking way.
        self.sync_queue.put_nowait(chat_completion_stream_responses_json_str)

    def handle_chat_completion(
        self, ffi: dict, request_json_str: str, include_usage: bool, request_id: str
    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
        """Helper class to handle chat completion

        Note
        ----
        ffi is explicitly passed in to avoid cylic dependency
        as ffi will capture EngineState
        """
        self.sync_queue = queue.Queue()

        ffi["chat_completion"](request_json_str, request_id)

        try:
            last_chunk_arrived = False
            while not last_chunk_arrived:
                chat_completion_responses_json_str = self.sync_queue.get()
                chat_completion_responses_list = json.loads(chat_completion_responses_json_str)
                for chat_completion_response_json_dict in chat_completion_responses_list:
                    chat_completion_response = (
                        openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse.model_validate(
                            chat_completion_response_json_dict
                        )
                    )
                    # the chunk with usage is always the last chunk
                    if chat_completion_response.usage is not None:
                        if include_usage:
                            yield chat_completion_response
                        last_chunk_arrived = True
                        break
                    yield chat_completion_response
        except Exception as exception:
            ffi["abort"](request_id)
            raise exception


class BackgroundLoops:
    """Helper class to keep track of background loops"""

    def __init__(self, ffi: dict):
        self._ffi = ffi
        # important: avoid self reference in closure
        background_loop = self._ffi["run_background_loop"]
        background_stream_back_loop = self._ffi["run_background_stream_back_loop"]

        # Create the background engine-driving thread and start the loop.
        self._background_loop_thread: threading.Thread = threading.Thread(target=background_loop)
        self._background_stream_back_loop_thread: threading.Thread = threading.Thread(
            target=background_stream_back_loop
        )
        self._background_loop_thread.start()
        self._background_stream_back_loop_thread.start()
        self._terminated = False

    def __del__(self):
        self.terminate()

    def terminate(self):
        if self._terminated:
            return
        self._terminated = True
        self._ffi["exit_background_loop"]()
        self._background_loop_thread.join()
        self._background_stream_back_loop_thread.join()


class Completions:
    """Completions class to be compatible with OpenAI API"""

    _ffi: dict
    _state: EngineState
    _background_loops: BackgroundLoops

    def __init__(self, ffi: dict, state: EngineState, background_loops: BackgroundLoops):
        self._ffi = ffi
        self._state = state
        self._background_loops = background_loops

    def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: bool = True,
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
        if request_id is None:
            request_id = f"chatcmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"
        debug_config = extra_body.get("debug_config", None) if extra_body is not None else None
        if not stream:
            raise ValueError("JSONFFIEngine only support stream=True")
        request = openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest(
            messages=[
                openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionMessage.model_validate(message)
                for message in messages
            ],
            model=model,
            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
            logprobs=logprobs,
            top_logprobs=top_logprobs,
            logit_bias=logit_bias,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            n=n,
            seed=seed,
            stop=stop,
            stream=stream,
            stream_options=(
                openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
                if stream_options is not None
                else None
            ),
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=top_p,
            tools=(
                [openai_api_protocol.ChatTool.model_validate(tool) for tool in tools]
                if tools is not None
                else None
            ),
            tool_choice=tool_choice,
            user=user,
            response_format=(
                openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
                if response_format is not None
                else None
            ),
            debug_config=(
                debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
                if debug_config is not None
                else None
            ),
        )
        chatcmpl_generator = self._state.handle_chat_completion(
            self._ffi,
            request.model_dump_json(by_alias=True),
            include_usage=(
                request.stream_options is not None and request.stream_options.include_usage
            ),
            request_id=request_id,
        )
        for response in chatcmpl_generator:
            yield response


class Chat:
    """Chat class to be compatible with OpenAI API"""

    completions: Completions

    def __init__(self, ffi: dict, state: EngineState, background_loops: BackgroundLoops):
        self.completions = Completions(ffi, state, background_loops)


class JSONFFIEngine:
    chat: Chat

    def __init__(
        self,
        model: str,
        device: Union[str, tvm.runtime.Device] = "auto",
        *,
        model_lib: Optional[str] = None,
        mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"] = "local",
        engine_config: Optional[EngineConfig] = None,
    ) -> None:
        # - Check the fields fields of `engine_config`.
        if engine_config is None:
            engine_config = EngineConfig()
        _check_engine_config(model, model_lib, mode, engine_config)

        # - Initialize model loading info.
        models = _parse_models(model, model_lib, engine_config.additional_models)
        if isinstance(device, str):
            device = detect_device(device)
        assert isinstance(device, tvm.runtime.Device)
        model_args = _process_model_args(models, device, engine_config)[0]

        # - Load the raw model config into dict
        for i, model_info in enumerate(models):
            model_info.model_lib = model_args[i][1]

        # - Initialize engine state and engine.
        self._state = EngineState()
        module = tvm.get_global_func("mlc.json_ffi.CreateJSONFFIEngine", allow_missing=False)()
        self._ffi = {
            key: module[key]
            for key in [
                "init_background_engine",
                "reload",
                "unload",
                "reset",
                "chat_completion",
                "abort",
                "run_background_loop",
                "run_background_stream_back_loop",
                "exit_background_loop",
            ]
        }
        self.tokenizer = Tokenizer(model_args[0][0])
        self._background_loops = BackgroundLoops(self._ffi)

        engine_config.model = model_args[0][0]
        engine_config.model_lib = model_args[0][1]
        engine_config.additional_models = model_args[1:]
        engine_config.mode = mode
        self.engine_config = engine_config

        self._ffi["init_background_engine"](
            device.dlpack_device_type(),
            device.index,
            self._state.get_request_stream_callback(),
        )
        self._ffi["reload"](self.engine_config.asjson())

        self.chat = Chat(self._ffi, self._state, self._background_loops)

    def metrics(self) -> EngineMetrics:
        """Get the engine metrics."""
        return _query_engine_metrics(self)

    def _raw_chat_completion(
        self, request_json_str: str, include_usage: bool, request_id: str
    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
        """Raw chat completion API"""
        return self._state.handle_chat_completion(
            self._ffi, request_json_str, include_usage, request_id
        )

    def terminate(self):
        """Explicitly terminate the engine"""
        self._background_loops.terminate()

    def _test_reload(self):
        self._ffi["reload"](self.engine_config.asjson())

    def _test_reset(self):
        self._ffi["reset"]()

    def _test_unload(self):
        self._ffi["unload"]()

819	class AsyncMLCEngine(engine_base.MLCEngineBase):
820	    """The AsyncMLCEngine in MLC LLM that provides the asynchronous
821	    interfaces with regard to OpenAI API.
822	
823	    Parameters
824	    ----------
825	    model : str
826	        A path to ``mlc-chat-config.json``, or an MLC model directory that contains
827	        `mlc-chat-config.json`.
828	        It can also be a link to a HF repository pointing to an MLC compiled model.
829	
830	    device: Union[str, Device]
831	        The device used to deploy the model such as "cuda" or "cuda:0".
832	        Will default to "auto" and detect from local available GPUs if not specified.
833	
834	    model_lib : Optional[str]
835	        The full path to the model library file to use (e.g. a ``.so`` file).
836	        If unspecified, we will use the provided ``model`` to search over possible paths.
837	        It the model lib is not found, it will be compiled in a JIT manner.
838	
839	    mode : Literal["local", "interactive", "server"]
840	        The engine mode in MLC LLM.
841	        We provide three preset modes: "local", "interactive" and "server".
842	        The default mode is "local".
843	        The choice of mode decides the values of "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length"
844	        and "prefill_chunk_size" when they are not explicitly specified.
845	        1. Mode "local" refers to the local server deployment which has low
846	        request concurrency. So the max batch size will be set to 4, and max
847	        total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
848	        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
849	        2. Mode "interactive" refers to the interactive use of server, which
850	        has at most 1 concurrent request. So the max batch size will be set to 1,
851	        and max total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
852	        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
853	        3. Mode "server" refers to the large server use case which may handle
854	        many concurrent request and want to use GPU memory as much as possible.
855	        In this mode, we will automatically infer the largest possible max batch
856	        size and max total sequence length.
857	
858	        You can manually specify arguments "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length" and
859	        "prefill_chunk_size" to override the automatic inferred values.
860	
861	    engine_config : Optional[EngineConfig]
862	        Additional configurable arguments of MLC engine.
863	        See class "EngineConfig" for more detail.
864

866	        A boolean indicating if to enable event logging for requests.

870	        self,
871	        model: str,
872	        device: Union[str, Device] = "auto",
873	        *,
874	        model_lib: Optional[str] = None,
875	        mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"] = "local",
876	        engine_config: Optional[EngineConfig] = None,
877	        enable_tracing: bool = False,
878	    ) -> None:
879	        super().__init__(

881	            model=model,
882	            device=device,
883	            model_lib=model_lib,
884	            mode=mode,
885	            engine_config=engine_config,
886	            enable_tracing=enable_tracing,
887	        )
888	        self.chat = AsyncChat(weakref.ref(self))
889	        self.completions = AsyncCompletion(weakref.ref(self))
890	
891	    async def abort(self, request_id: str) -> None:
892	        """Generation abortion interface.
893	
894	        Parameters
895	        ---------
896	        request_id : str
897	            The id of the request to abort.

909	        return await engine_base._async_query_engine_metrics(self)

950	
951	        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
952	            Debug config body options to pass to the request.
953	
954	        Raises
955	        ------
956	        e : BadRequestError
957	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
958	        """
959	        if request_id is None:
960	            request_id = f"chatcmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"

962	        chatcmpl_generator = self._handle_chat_completion(
963	            openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest(
964	                messages=[
965	                    openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionMessage.model_validate(message)
966	                    for message in messages
967	                ],
968	                model=model,
969	                frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
970	                presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
971	                logprobs=logprobs,
972	                top_logprobs=top_logprobs,
973	                logit_bias=logit_bias,
974	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
975	                n=n,
976	                seed=seed,
977	                stop=stop,
978	                stream=stream,
979	                stream_options=(
980	                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
981	                    if stream_options is not None
982	                    else None
983	                ),
984	                temperature=temperature,
985	                top_p=top_p,
986	                tools=(
987	                    [openai_api_protocol.ChatTool.model_validate(tool) for tool in tools]
988	                    if tools is not None
989	                    else None
990	                ),
991	                tool_choice=tool_choice,
992	                user=user,
993	                response_format=(
994	                    openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
995	                    if response_format is not None
996	                    else None
997	                ),
998	                debug_config=(
999	                    debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
1000	                    if debug_config is not None
1001	                    else None
1002	                ),
1003	            ),
1004	            request_id=request_id,
1005	            request_final_usage_include_extra=True,
1006	        )
1007	        if stream:
1008	            # Stream response.
1009	            return chatcmpl_generator
1010	        # Normal response.
1011	        output_texts = ["" for _ in range(n)]
1012	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(n)]  # noqa: UP006
1013	        logprob_results: Optional[List[List[openai_api_protocol.LogProbsContent]]] = (  # noqa: UP006
1014	            [[] for _ in range(n)] if logprobs else None
1015	        )
1016	        request_final_usage = None

1021	                    request_final_usage = response.usage
1022	                    continue
1023	                for choice in response.choices:
1024	                    assert isinstance(choice.delta.content, str)
1025	                    output_texts[choice.index] += choice.delta.content

1032	            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
1033	            raise
1034	        except Exception as err:
1035	            logger.error("Error in chat completion with request ID %s: %s", request_id, err)
1036	            raise
1037	
1038	        assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)
1039	        use_function_calling, tool_calls_list = engine_base.process_function_call_output(
1040	            output_texts, finish_reasons
1041	        )
1042	        return engine_base.wrap_chat_completion_response(
1043	            request_id=request_id,
1044	            model=model,
1045	            output_texts=output_texts,
1046	            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
1047	            tool_calls_list=tool_calls_list,
1048	            logprob_results=logprob_results,
1049	            use_function_calling=use_function_calling,

1171	    async def _handle_chat_completion(
1172	        self,
1173	        request: openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest,
1174	        request_id: str,
1175	        request_final_usage_include_extra: bool,
1176	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any]:
1177	        """The implementation fo asynchronous ChatCompletionRequest handling.
1178	
1179	        Yields
1180	        ------
1181	        stream_response : ChatCompletionStreamResponse
1182	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1183	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1184	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming for specification.
1185	
1186	        Raises
1187	        ------
1188	        e : BadRequestError
1189	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1190	        """
1191	        (
1192	            prompts,
1193	            generation_cfg,
1194	            use_function_calling,
1195	            prompt_length,
1196	        ) = engine_base.process_chat_completion_request(
1197	            request,
1198	            request_id,
1199	            self.state,
1200	            self.model_config_dicts[0],
1201	            self.tokenizer.encode,
1202	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1203	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1204	        )
1205	        # prompt length is not used
1206	        _ = prompt_length
1207	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(generation_cfg.n)]  # noqa: UP006
1208	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1209	        try:
1210	            async for delta_outputs in self._generate(
1211	                prompts,
1212	                generation_cfg,
1213	                request_id,
1214	            ):
1215	                response = engine_base.process_chat_completion_stream_output(
1216	                    delta_outputs,
1217	                    request,
1218	                    request_id,
1219	                    self.state,
1220	                    use_function_calling,
1221	                    finish_reasons,
1222	                )
1223	
1224	                if response is not None:
1225	                    if response.usage is not None:
1226	                        if not request_final_usage_include_extra:
1227	                            response.usage.extra = None
1228	                    yield response
1229	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1230	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
1231	            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
1232	            raise
1233	        except Exception as err:
1234	            logger.error("Error in _handle_chat_completion for request %s: %s", request_id, err)
1235	            raise

1237	    async def _handle_completion(
1238	        self,
1239	        request: openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest,
1240	        request_id: str,
1241	        request_final_usage_include_extra: bool,
1242	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse, Any]:
1243	        """The implementation fo asynchronous CompletionRequest handling.
1244	
1245	        Yields
1246	        ------
1247	        stream_response : CompletionResponse
1248	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1249	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1250	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/object for specification.
1251	
1252	        Raises
1253	        ------
1254	        e : BadRequestError
1255	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1256	        """
1257	        (
1258	            prompt,
1259	            generation_cfg,
1260	            prompt_length,
1261	            echo_response,
1262	        ) = engine_base.process_completion_request(
1263	            request,
1264	            request_id,
1265	            self.state,
1266	            self.tokenizer,
1267	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1268	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1269	        )
1270	        _ = prompt_length
1271	        if echo_response is not None:
1272	            yield echo_response
1273	
1274	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None] * generation_cfg.n  # noqa: UP006
1275	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1276	        try:
1277	            async for delta_outputs in self._generate(
1278	                prompt,
1279	                generation_cfg,
1280	                request_id,
1281	            ):
1282	                response = engine_base.process_completion_stream_output(
1283	                    delta_outputs,
1284	                    request,
1285	                    request_id,
1286	                    self.state,
1287	                    finish_reasons,
1288	                )
1289	
1290	                if response is not None:
1291	                    if response.usage is not None:
1292	                        if not request_final_usage_include_extra:
1293	                            response.usage.extra = None
1294	                    yield response
1295	
1296	            suffix_response = engine_base.create_completion_suffix_response(
1297	                request, request_id, finish_reasons
1298	            )
1299	            if suffix_response is not None:
1300	                yield suffix_response
1301	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1302	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
1303	            # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
1304	            raise
1305	        except Exception as err:
1306	            logger.error("Error in _handle_completion for request %s: %s", request_id, err)
1307	            raise

1309	    async def _generate(
1310	        self,
1311	        prompt: Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]],  # noqa: UP006
1312	        generation_config: GenerationConfig,
1313	        request_id: str,
1314	    ) -> AsyncGenerator[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput], Any]:  # noqa: UP006
1315	        """Internal asynchronous text generation interface of AsyncMLCEngine.
1316	        The method is a coroutine that streams a list of CallbackStreamOutput
1317	        at a time via yield. The returned list length is the number of
1318	        parallel generations specified by `generation_config.n`.
1319	
1320	        Parameters
1321	        ----------
1322	        prompt : Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]]
1323	            The input prompt in forms of text strings, lists of token ids or data.
1324	
1325	        generation_config : GenerationConfig
1326	            The generation config of the request.
1327	
1328	        request_id : str
1329	            The unique identifier (in string) or this generation request.
1330	
1331	        Yields
1332	        ------
1333	        request_output : List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]
1334	            The delta generated outputs in a list.
1335	            The number of list elements equals to `generation_config.n`,
1336	            and each element corresponds to the delta output of a parallel
1337	            generation.
1338	        """
1339	        if self._terminated:
1340	            raise ValueError("The AsyncThreadedEngine has terminated.")
1341	        self.state.async_lazy_init_event_loop()
1342	
1343	        # Create the request with the given id, input data, generation
1344	        # config and the created callback.
1345	        input_data = engine_utils.convert_prompts_to_data(prompt)
1346	        request = self._ffi["create_request"](
1347	            request_id, input_data, generation_config.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)
1348	        )
1349	
1350	        # Create the unique async request stream of the request.
1351	        stream = engine_base.AsyncRequestStream()
1352	        if request_id in self.state.async_streamers:
1353	            # Report error in the stream if the request id already exists.
1354	            stream.push(
1355	                RuntimeError(
1356	                    f'The request id "{request_id} already exists. '
1357	                    'Please make sure the request id is unique."'
1358	                )
1359	            )
1360	        else:
1361	            # Record the stream in the tracker
1362	            self.state.async_streamers[request_id] = (
1363	                stream,
1364	                [TextStreamer(self.tokenizer) for _ in range(generation_config.n)],
1365	            )
1366	            self._ffi["add_request"](request)
1367	
1368	        def abort_request():
1369	            """clean up"""
1370	            self._abort(request_id)
1371	            logger.info("request %s cancelled", request_id)
1372	
1373	        with engine_utils.ErrorCleanupScope(abort_request):
1374	            # Iterate the stream asynchronously and yield the output.
1375	            try:
1376	                async for request_output in stream:
1377	                    yield request_output
1378	            except asyncio.CancelledError:
1379	                # for cancelled error, we can simply pass it through
1380	                raise
1381	            except Exception as exception:
1382	                logger.error("Exception in _generate for request %s: %s", request_id, exception)
1383	                raise
1384	
1385	    def _abort(self, request_id: str):
1386	        """Internal implementation of request abortion."""
1387	        self.state.async_streamers.pop(request_id, None)
1388	        self._ffi["abort_request"](request_id)
1389

1391	class MLCEngine(engine_base.MLCEngineBase):
1392	    """The MLCEngine in MLC LLM that provides the synchronous
1393	    interfaces with regard to OpenAI API.
1394	
1395	    Parameters
1396	    ----------
1397	    model : str
1398	        A path to ``mlc-chat-config.json``, or an MLC model directory that contains
1399	        `mlc-chat-config.json`.
1400	        It can also be a link to a HF repository pointing to an MLC compiled model.
1401	
1402	    device: Union[str, Device]
1403	        The device used to deploy the model such as "cuda" or "cuda:0".
1404	        Will default to "auto" and detect from local available GPUs if not specified.
1405	
1406	    model_lib : Optional[str]
1407	        The full path to the model library file to use (e.g. a ``.so`` file).
1408	        If unspecified, we will use the provided ``model`` to search over possible paths.
1409	        It the model lib is not found, it will be compiled in a JIT manner.
1410	
1411	    mode : Literal["local", "interactive", "server"]
1412	        The engine mode in MLC LLM.
1413	        We provide three preset modes: "local", "interactive" and "server".
1414	        The default mode is "local".
1415	        The choice of mode decides the values of "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length"
1416	        and "prefill_chunk_size" when they are not explicitly specified.
1417	        1. Mode "local" refers to the local server deployment which has low
1418	        request concurrency. So the max batch size will be set to 4, and max
1419	        total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
1420	        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
1421	        2. Mode "interactive" refers to the interactive use of server, which
1422	        has at most 1 concurrent request. So the max batch size will be set to 1,
1423	        and max total sequence length and prefill chunk size are set to the context
1424	        window size (or sliding window size) of the model.
1425	        3. Mode "server" refers to the large server use case which may handle
1426	        many concurrent request and want to use GPU memory as much as possible.
1427	        In this mode, we will automatically infer the largest possible max batch
1428	        size and max total sequence length.
1429	
1430	        You can manually specify arguments "max_num_sequence", "max_total_sequence_length" and
1431	        "prefill_chunk_size" to override the automatic inferred values.
1432	
1433	    engine_config : Optional[EngineConfig]
1434	        Additional configurable arguments of MLC engine.
1435	        See class "EngineConfig" for more detail.
1436	
1437	    enable_tracing : bool
1438	        A boolean indicating if to enable event logging for requests.
1439	    """
1440	
1441	    def __init__(
1442	        self,
1443	        model: str,
1444	        device: Union[str, Device] = "auto",
1445	        *,
1446	        model_lib: Optional[str] = None,
1447	        mode: Literal["local", "interactive", "server"] = "local",
1448	        engine_config: Optional[EngineConfig] = None,
1449	        enable_tracing: bool = False,
1450	    ) -> None:
1451	        super().__init__(
1452	            "sync",
1453	            model=model,
1454	            device=device,
1455	            model_lib=model_lib,
1456	            mode=mode,
1457	            engine_config=engine_config,
1458	            enable_tracing=enable_tracing,
1459	        )
1460	        self.chat = Chat(weakref.ref(self))
1461	        self.completions = Completion(weakref.ref(self))
1462	
1463	    def abort(self, request_id: str) -> None:
1464	        """Generation abortion interface.
1465	
1466	        Parameters
1467	        ---------
1468	        request_id : str
1469	            The id of the request to abort.
1470	        """
1471	        self._ffi["abort_request"](request_id)
1472	
1473	    def metrics(self) -> engine_base.EngineMetrics:
1474	        """Get engine metrics
1475	
1476	        Returns
1477	        -------
1478	        metrics: EngineMetrics
1479	            The engine metrics
1480	        """
1481	        return engine_base._query_engine_metrics(self)

1535	                    openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionMessage.model_validate(message)
1536	                    for message in messages
1537	                ],
1538	                model=model,
1539	                frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
1540	                presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
1541	                logprobs=logprobs,
1542	                top_logprobs=top_logprobs,
1543	                logit_bias=logit_bias,
1544	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
1545	                n=n,
1546	                seed=seed,
1547	                stop=stop,
1548	                stream=stream,
1549	                stream_options=(
1550	                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
1551	                    if stream_options is not None
1552	                    else None
1553	                ),
1554	                temperature=temperature,
1555	                top_p=top_p,
1556	                tools=(
1557	                    [openai_api_protocol.ChatTool.model_validate(tool) for tool in tools]
1558	                    if tools is not None
1559	                    else None
1560	                ),
1561	                tool_choice=tool_choice,
1562	                user=user,
1563	                response_format=(
1564	                    openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
1565	                    if response_format is not None
1566	                    else None
1567	                ),
1568	                debug_config=(
1569	                    debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
1570	                    if debug_config is not None
1571	                    else None
1572	                ),

1575	        )
1576	        if stream:

1579	        # Normal response.
1580	        request_final_usage = None
1581	        output_texts = ["" for _ in range(n)]
1582	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(n)]  # noqa: UP006
1583	        logprob_results: Optional[List[List[openai_api_protocol.LogProbsContent]]] = (  # noqa: UP006

1595	                    finish_reasons[choice.index] = choice.finish_reason
1596	                if choice.logprobs is not None:
1597	                    assert logprob_results is not None
1598	                    logprob_results[choice.index] += choice.logprobs.content
1599	
1600	        assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)
1601	        use_function_calling, tool_calls_list = engine_base.process_function_call_output(
1602	            output_texts, finish_reasons
1603	        )
1604	        return engine_base.wrap_chat_completion_response(
1605	            request_id=request_id,
1606	            model=model,
1607	            output_texts=output_texts,
1608	            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
1609	            tool_calls_list=tool_calls_list,
1610	            logprob_results=logprob_results,
1611	            use_function_calling=use_function_calling,
1612	            usage=request_final_usage,
1613	        )

1615	    def _completion(
1616	        self,
1617	        *,
1618	        prompt: Union[str, List[int]],  # noqa: UP006
1619	        model: Optional[str] = None,
1620	        best_of: int = 1,
1621	        echo: bool = False,
1622	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
1623	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
1624	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
1625	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1626	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
1627	        n: int = 1,
1628	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
1629	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1630	        stream: bool = False,
1631	        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1632	        suffix: Optional[str] = None,
1633	        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
1634	        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
1635	        user: Optional[str] = None,
1636	        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1637	        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
1638	        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
1639	    ) -> Union[
1640	        Iterator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse],
1641	        openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse,
1642	    ]:
1643	        """Synchronous completion internal interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
1644	
1645	        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create for specification.
1646	
1647	        Parameters
1648	        ----------
1649	        request_id : Optional[str]
1650	            The optional request id.
1651	            A random one will be generated if it is not given.
1652	
1653	        debug_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
1654	            Extra debug options to pass to the request.
1655	
1656	        Raises
1657	        ------
1658	        e : BadRequestError
1659	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1660	        """
1661	        if request_id is None:
1662	            request_id = f"cmpl-{engine_utils.random_uuid()}"
1663	
1664	        cmpl_generator = self._handle_completion(
1665	            openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest(
1666	                model=model,
1667	                prompt=prompt,
1668	                best_of=best_of,
1669	                echo=echo,
1670	                frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
1671	                presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
1672	                logprobs=logprobs,
1673	                logit_bias=logit_bias,
1674	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
1675	                n=n,
1676	                seed=seed,
1677	                stop=stop,
1678	                stream=stream,
1679	                stream_options=(
1680	                    openai_api_protocol.StreamOptions.model_validate(stream_options)
1681	                    if stream_options is not None
1682	                    else None
1683	                ),
1684	                suffix=suffix,
1685	                temperature=temperature,
1686	                top_p=top_p,
1687	                user=user,
1688	                response_format=(
1689	                    openai_api_protocol.RequestResponseFormat.model_validate(response_format)
1690	                    if response_format is not None
1691	                    else None
1692	                ),
1693	                debug_config=(
1694	                    debug_protocol.DebugConfig.model_validate(debug_config)
1695	                    if debug_config is not None
1696	                    else None
1697	                ),
1698	            ),
1699	            request_id=request_id,
1700	        )
1701	        if stream:
1702	            # Stream response.
1703	            return cmpl_generator
1704	        # Normal response.
1705	        request_final_usage = None
1706	        output_texts = [""] * n
1707	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None] * n  # noqa: UP006
1708	        logprob_results: List[Optional[openai_api_protocol.CompletionLogProbs]] = [None] * n  # noqa: UP006
1709	
1710	        for response in cmpl_generator:
1711	            # this is the final chunk
1712	            if response.usage is not None:
1713	                request_final_usage = response.usage
1714	                continue
1715	            for choice in response.choices:
1716	                output_texts[choice.index] += choice.text
1717	                if choice.finish_reason is not None and finish_reasons[choice.index] is None:
1718	                    finish_reasons[choice.index] = choice.finish_reason
1719	                if choice.logprobs is not None:
1720	                    logprob_results[choice.index] = choice.logprobs
1721	
1722	        assert all(finish_reason is not None for finish_reason in finish_reasons)
1723	        return engine_base.wrap_completion_response(
1724	            request_id=request_id,
1725	            model=model,
1726	            output_texts=output_texts,
1727	            finish_reasons=finish_reasons,
1728	            logprob_results=logprob_results,
1729	            usage=request_final_usage,
1730	        )
1731	
1732	    def _handle_chat_completion(
1733	        self, request: openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionRequest, request_id: str
1734	    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse]:
1735	        """The implementation fo synchronous ChatCompletionRequest handling.
1736	
1737	        Yields
1738	        ------
1739	        stream_response : CompletionResponse
1740	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1741	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1742	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming for specification.
1743	
1744	        Raises
1745	        ------
1746	        e : BadRequestError
1747	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1748	        """
1749	        (
1750	            prompts,
1751	            generation_cfg,
1752	            use_function_calling,
1753	            prompt_length,
1754	        ) = engine_base.process_chat_completion_request(
1755	            request,
1756	            request_id,
1757	            self.state,
1758	            self.model_config_dicts[0],
1759	            self.tokenizer.encode,
1760	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1761	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1762	        )
1763	        _ = prompt_length
1764	
1765	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(generation_cfg.n)]  # noqa: UP006
1766	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1767	        for delta_outputs in self._generate(prompts, generation_cfg, request_id):
1768	            response = engine_base.process_chat_completion_stream_output(
1769	                delta_outputs,
1770	                request,
1771	                request_id,
1772	                self.state,
1773	                use_function_calling,
1774	                finish_reasons,
1775	            )
1776	            if response is not None:
1777	                yield response
1778	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1779	
1780	    def _handle_completion(
1781	        self, request: openai_api_protocol.CompletionRequest, request_id: str
1782	    ) -> Iterator[openai_api_protocol.CompletionResponse]:
1783	        """The implementation for synchronous CompletionRequest handling.
1784	
1785	        Yields
1786	        ------
1787	        stream_response : CompletionResponse
1788	            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
1789	            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
1790	            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/object for specification.
1791	
1792	        Raises
1793	        ------
1794	        e : BadRequestError
1795	            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
1796	        """
1797	        (
1798	            prompt,
1799	            generation_cfg,
1800	            prompt_length,
1801	            echo_response,
1802	        ) = engine_base.process_completion_request(
1803	            request,
1804	            request_id,
1805	            self.state,
1806	            self.tokenizer,
1807	            self.max_input_sequence_length,
1808	            self.conv_template.model_copy(deep=True),
1809	        )
1810	        _ = prompt_length
1811	        if echo_response is not None:
1812	            yield echo_response
1813	
1814	        finish_reasons: List[Optional[str]] = [None for _ in range(generation_cfg.n)]  # noqa: UP006
1815	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="invoke generate")
1816	        for delta_outputs in self._generate(prompt, generation_cfg, request_id):
1817	            response = engine_base.process_completion_stream_output(
1818	                delta_outputs,
1819	                request,
1820	                request_id,
1821	                self.state,
1822	                finish_reasons,
1823	            )
1824	            if response is not None:
1825	                yield response
1826	
1827	        suffix_response = engine_base.create_completion_suffix_response(
1828	            request, request_id, finish_reasons
1829	        )
1830	        if suffix_response is not None:
1831	            yield suffix_response
1832	        self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish")
1833	
1834	    def _generate(
1835	        self,
1836	        prompt: Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]],  # noqa: UP006
1837	        generation_config: GenerationConfig,
1838	        request_id: str,
1839	    ) -> Iterator[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]]:  # noqa: UP006
1840	        """Internal synchronous text generation interface of MLCEngine.
1841	        The method is a coroutine that streams a list of CallbackStreamOutput
1842	        at a time via yield. The returned list length is the number of
1843	        parallel generations specified by `generation_config.n`
1844	        except for the final chunk(which is always an List of size 1 and comes with usage)
1845	
1846	        Parameters
1847	        ----------
1848	        prompt : Union[str, List[int], List[Union[str, List[int], data.Data]]]
1849	            The input prompt in forms of text strings, lists of token ids or data.
1850	
1851	        generation_config : GenerationConfig
1852	            The generation config of the request.
1853	
1854	        request_id : str
1855	            The unique identifier (in string) or this generation request.
1856	
1857	        Yields
1858	        ------
1859	        request_output : List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]
1860	            The delta generated outputs in a list.
1861	            Except for the final chunk, the number of list elements equals to `generation_config.n`,
1862	            and each element corresponds to the delta output of a parallel generation.
1863	        """
1864	        if self._terminated:
1865	            raise ValueError("The engine has terminated.")
1866	
1867	        # Create the request with the given id, input data, generation
1868	        # config and the created callback.
1869	        input_data = engine_utils.convert_prompts_to_data(prompt)
1870	        request = self._ffi["create_request"](
1871	            request_id, input_data, generation_config.model_dump_json(by_alias=True)
1872	        )
1873	
1874	        # Record the stream in the tracker
1875	        self.state.sync_output_queue = queue.Queue()
1876	        self.state.sync_text_streamers = [
1877	            TextStreamer(self.tokenizer) for _ in range(generation_config.n)
1878	        ]
1879	        self._ffi["add_request"](request)
1880	
1881	        def abort_request():
1882	            """clean up request if exception happens"""
1883	            self.abort(request_id)
1884	
1885	        # Iterate the stream asynchronously and yield the token.
1886	        with engine_utils.ErrorCleanupScope(abort_request):
1887	            while True:
1888	                delta_outputs = self.state.sync_output_queue.get()
1889	                request_outputs, request_final_usage_json_str = self._request_stream_callback_impl(
1890	                    delta_outputs
1891	                )
1892	                for request_output in request_outputs:
1893	                    yield request_output
1894	
1895	                if request_final_usage_json_str is not None:
1896	                    # final chunk, we can break
1897	                    output = engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput(
1898	                        delta_text="",
1899	                        delta_logprob_json_strs=None,
1900	                        finish_reason=None,
1901	                        request_final_usage_json_str=request_final_usage_json_str,
1902	                    )
1903	                    yield [output]
1904	                    break
1905	
1906	    def _request_stream_callback_impl(
1907	        self,
1908	        delta_outputs: List[data.RequestStreamOutput],  # noqa: UP006
1909	    ) -> Tuple[List[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]], Optional[str]]:  # noqa: UP006
1910	        """The underlying implementation of request stream callback of MLCEngine."""
1911	        batch_outputs: List[List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput]] = []  # noqa: UP006
1912	        for delta_output in delta_outputs:
1913	            request_id, stream_outputs = delta_output.unpack()
1914	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="start callback")
1915	
1916	            # final chunk is now always indicated by a chunk
1917	            # where usage json is present
1918	            # the backend engine always streams back this chunk
1919	            # regardless of include_usage option
1920	            is_final_chunk = stream_outputs[0].request_final_usage_json_str is not None
1921	            if is_final_chunk:
1922	                return (batch_outputs, stream_outputs[0].request_final_usage_json_str)
1923	
1924	            outputs: List[engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput] = []  # noqa: UP006
1925	            for stream_output, text_streamer in zip(stream_outputs, self.state.sync_text_streamers):
1926	                self.state.record_event(request_id, event="start detokenization")
1927	                delta_text = stream_output.extra_prefix_string + (
1928	                    text_streamer.put(stream_output.delta_token_ids)
1929	                    if len(stream_output.delta_token_ids) > 0
1930	                    else ""
1931	                )
1932	                if stream_output.finish_reason is not None:
1933	                    delta_text += text_streamer.finish()
1934	                self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish detokenization")
1935	
1936	                outputs.append(
1937	                    engine_base.CallbackStreamOutput(
1938	                        delta_text=delta_text,
1939	                        delta_logprob_json_strs=stream_output.delta_logprob_json_strs,
1940	                        finish_reason=stream_output.finish_reason,
1941	                        request_final_usage_json_str=None,
1942	                    )
1943	                )
1944	            batch_outputs.append(outputs)
1945	            self.state.record_event(request_id, event="finish callback")
1946	        return (batch_outputs, None)
1947

"""The MLC LLM Serving Engine."""

import asyncio
import queue
import weakref
from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator, Iterator
from typing import (  # noqa: UP035
    Any,
    Dict,
    List,
    Literal,
    Optional,
    Tuple,
    Union,
    overload,
)

from tvm.runtime import Device

from mlc_llm.protocol import debug_protocol, openai_api_protocol
from mlc_llm.protocol.generation_config import GenerationConfig
from mlc_llm.serve import data, engine_utils
from mlc_llm.serve.config import EngineConfig
from mlc_llm.support import logging
from mlc_llm.tokenizers import TextStreamer

from . import engine_base

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# Note: we define both AsyncChat and Chat for Python type analysis.
class AsyncChat:
    """The proxy class to direct to async chat completions."""

    def __init__(self, engine: weakref.ReferenceType) -> None:
        assert isinstance(engine(), AsyncMLCEngine)
        self.completions = AsyncChatCompletion(engine)


class Chat:
    """The proxy class to direct to chat completions."""

    def __init__(self, engine: weakref.ReferenceType) -> None:
        assert isinstance(engine(), MLCEngine)
        self.completions = ChatCompletion(engine)


class AsyncChatCompletion:
    """The proxy class to direct to async chat completions."""

    engine: weakref.ReferenceType["AsyncMLCEngine"]

    def __init__(self, engine: weakref.ReferenceType) -> None:
        self.engine = engine

    @overload
    async def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        stream: Literal[True],
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any]:
        """Asynchronous streaming chat completion interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
        The method is a coroutine that streams ChatCompletionStreamResponse
        that conforms to OpenAI API one at a time via yield.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra body options to pass to the request.
            Can be used to pass debug config as extra_body["debug_config"]

        Yields
        ------
        stream_response : ChatCompletionStreamResponse
            The stream response conforming to OpenAI API.
            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming for specification.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """

    @overload
    async def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: Literal[False] = False,
        stream_options: Literal[None] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionResponse:
        """Asynchronous non-streaming chat completion interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
        The method is a coroutine that streams ChatCompletionStreamResponse
        that conforms to OpenAI API one at a time via yield.

        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create for specification.

        Parameters
        ----------
        request_id : Optional[str]
            The optional request id.
            A random one will be generated if it is not given.

        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
            Extra body options to pass to the request.
            Can be used to pass debug config as extra_body["debug_config"]

        Returns
        -------
        response : ChatCompletionResponse
            The chat completion response conforming to OpenAI API.
            See mlc_llm/protocol/openai_api_protocol.py or
            https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/object for specification.

        Raises
        ------
        e : BadRequestError
            BadRequestError is raised when the request is invalid.
        """

    async def create(
        self,
        *,
        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],  # noqa: UP006
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        logprobs: bool = False,
        top_logprobs: int = 0,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: int = 1,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        stream: bool = False,
        stream_options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        tool_choice: Optional[Union[Literal["none", "auto"], Dict]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        user: Optional[str] = None,
        response_format: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
        request_id: Optional[str] = None,
        extra_body: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,  # noqa: UP006
    ) -> Union[
        AsyncGenerator[openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionStreamResponse, Any],
        openai_api_protocol.ChatCompletionResponse,
    ]:
        """Asynchronous chat completion interface with OpenAI API compatibility.
